title: More Nonsense Limerick 48
content:
There was an old man of Spithead,
    Who opened the window, and said, - 
    "Fil-jomble, fil-jumble, 
    Fil-rumble-come-tumble!"
    That doubtful old man of Spithead.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Edward Lear delivers a powerful performance in "More Nonsense Limerick 48"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
